lines.gurmukhi: ਨੰਗਾ ਦੋਜਕਿ ਚਾਲਿਆ ਤਾ ਦਿਸੈ ਖਰਾ ਡਰਾਵਣਾ ॥
lines.translit: nangaa dojak chaaliaa taa disai kharaa ddaraavanaa |
lines.english: He goes to hell naked, and he looks hideous then.
He regrets the sins he committed. ||14||

lines.gurmukhi: ਏਹੁ ਜਨੇਊ ਜੀਅ ਕਾ ਹਈ ਤ ਪਾਡੇ ਘਤੁ ॥
lines.translit: ehu janeaoo jeea kaa hee ta paadde ghat |
lines.english: This is the sacred thread of the soul; if you have it, then go ahead and put it on me.
Salok, First Mehl:

lines.gurmukhi: ਦਇਆ ਕਪਾਹ ਸੰਤੋਖੁ ਸੂਤੁ ਜਤੁ ਗੰਢੀ ਸਤੁ ਵਟੁ ॥
lines.translit: deaa kapaah santokh soot jat gandtee sat vatt |
lines.english: Make compassion the cotton, contentment the thread, modesty the knot and truth the twist.

lines.gurmukhi: ਧੰਨੁ ਸੁ ਮਾਣਸ ਨਾਨਕਾ ਜੋ ਗਲਿ ਚਲੇ ਪਾਇ ॥
lines.translit: dhan su maanas naanakaa jo gal chale paae |
lines.english: Blessed are those mortal beings, O Nanak, who wear such a thread around their necks.

lines.gurmukhi: ਨਾ ਏਹੁ ਤੁਟੈ ਨ ਮਲੁ ਲਗੈ ਨਾ ਏਹੁ ਜਲੈ ਨ ਜਾਇ ॥
lines.translit: naa ehu tuttai na mal lagai naa ehu jalai na jaae |
lines.english: It does not break, it cannot be soiled by filth, it cannot be burnt, or lost.

lines.gurmukhi: ਸਿਖਾ ਕੰਨਿ ਚੜਾਈਆ ਗੁਰੁ ਬ੍ਰਾਹਮਣੁ ਥਿਆ ॥
lines.translit: sikhaa kan charraaeea gur braahaman thiaa |
lines.english: Whispering instructions into others' ears, the Brahmin becomes a guru.

lines.gurmukhi: ਚਉਕੜਿ ਮੁਲਿ ਅਣਾਇਆ ਬਹਿ ਚਉਕੈ ਪਾਇਆ ॥
lines.translit: chaukarr mul anaaeaa beh chaukai paaeaa |
lines.english: You buy the thread for a few shells, and seated in your enclosure, you put it on.

lines.gurmukhi: ਓਹੁ ਮੁਆ ਓਹੁ ਝੜਿ ਪਇਆ ਵੇਤਗਾ ਗਇਆ ॥੧॥
lines.translit: ohu muaa ohu jharr peaa vetagaa geaa |1|
lines.english: But he dies, and the sacred thread falls away, and the soul departs without it. ||1||
First Mehl:

lines.gurmukhi: ਲਖ ਚੋਰੀਆ ਲਖ ਜਾਰੀਆ ਲਖ ਕੂੜੀਆ ਲਖ ਗਾਲਿ ॥
lines.translit: lakh choreea lakh jaareea lakh koorreea lakh gaal |
lines.english: He commits thousands of robberies, thousands of acts of adultery, thousands of falsehoods and thousands of abuses.

lines.gurmukhi: ਤਗੁ ਕਪਾਹਹੁ ਕਤੀਐ ਬਾਮੑਣੁ ਵਟੇ ਆਇ ॥
lines.translit: tag kapaahahu kateeai baaman vatte aae |
lines.english: The thread is spun from cotton, and the Brahmin comes and twists it.

lines.gurmukhi: ਲਖ ਠਗੀਆ ਪਹਿਨਾਮੀਆ ਰਾਤਿ ਦਿਨਸੁ ਜੀਅ ਨਾਲਿ ॥
lines.translit: lakh tthageea pahinaameea raat dinas jeea naal |
lines.english: He practices thousands of deceptions and secret deeds, night and day, against his fellow beings.

lines.gurmukhi: ਹੋਇ ਪੁਰਾਣਾ ਸੁਟੀਐ ਭੀ ਫਿਰਿ ਪਾਈਐ ਹੋਰੁ ॥
lines.translit: hoe puraanaa sutteeai bhee fir paaeeai hor |
lines.english: When it wears out, it is thrown away, and another one is put on.

lines.gurmukhi: ਕੁਹਿ ਬਕਰਾ ਰਿੰਨੑਿ ਖਾਇਆ ਸਭੁ ਕੋ ਆਖੈ ਪਾਇ ॥
lines.translit: kuhi bakaraa rina khaaeaa sabh ko aakhai paae |
lines.english: The goat is killed, cooked and eaten, and everyone then says, "Put on the sacred thread."

lines.gurmukhi: ਨਾਨਕ ਤਗੁ ਨ ਤੁਟਈ ਜੇ ਤਗਿ ਹੋਵੈ ਜੋਰੁ ॥੨॥
lines.translit: naanak tag na tuttee je tag hovai jor |2|
lines.english: O Nanak, the thread would not break, if it had any real strength. ||2||
First Mehl:

lines.gurmukhi: ਨਾਇ ਮੰਨਿਐ ਪਤਿ ਊਪਜੈ ਸਾਲਾਹੀ ਸਚੁ ਸੂਤੁ ॥
lines.translit: naae maniai pat aoopajai saalaahee sach soot |
lines.english: Believing in the Name, honor is obtained. The Lord's Praise is the true sacred thread.
First Mehl:
The man's beard is spat upon daily.

lines.gurmukhi: ਤਗੁ ਨ ਇੰਦ੍ਰੀ ਤਗੁ ਨ ਨਾਰੀ ॥
lines.translit: tag na indree tag na naaree |
lines.english: There is no sacred thread for the sexual organ, and no thread for woman.

lines.gurmukhi: ਦਰਗਹ ਅੰਦਰਿ ਪਾਈਐ ਤਗੁ ਨ ਤੂਟਸਿ ਪੂਤ ॥੩॥
lines.translit: daragah andar paaeeai tag na toottas poot |3|
lines.english: Such a sacred thread is worn in the Court of the Lord; it shall never break. ||3||
The Brahmin himself goes to the world hereafter without a sacred thread.
Twisting the threads, he puts them on others.

lines.gurmukhi: ਤਗੁ ਨ ਜਿਹਵਾ ਤਗੁ ਨ ਅਖੀ ॥
lines.translit: tag na jihavaa tag na akhee |
lines.english: no thread for the tongue, and no thread for the eyes.

lines.gurmukhi: ਤਗੁ ਨ ਪੈਰੀ ਤਗੁ ਨ ਹਥੀ ॥
lines.translit: tag na pairee tag na hathee |
lines.english: There is no sacred thread for the feet, and no thread for the hands;
Hear, and see, O people, this wondrous thing.
He takes payment for performing marriages;
reading their horoscopes, he shows them the way.
He is mentally blind, and yet his name is wisdom. ||4||
Pauree:

lines.gurmukhi: ਸਾਹਿਬੁ ਹੋਇ ਦਇਆਲੁ ਕਿਰਪਾ ਕਰੇ ਤਾ ਸਾਈ ਕਾਰ ਕਰਾਇਸੀ ॥
lines.translit: saahib hoe deaal kirapaa kare taa saaee kaar karaaeisee |
lines.english: One, upon whom the Merciful Lord bestows His Grace, performs His service.

lines.gurmukhi: ਸੋ ਸੇਵਕੁ ਸੇਵਾ ਕਰੇ ਜਿਸ ਨੋ ਹੁਕਮੁ ਮਨਾਇਸੀ ॥
lines.translit: so sevak sevaa kare jis no hukam manaaeisee |
lines.english: That servant, whom the Lord causes to obey the Order of His Will, serves Him.

lines.gurmukhi: ਹੁਕਮਿ ਮੰਨਿਐ ਹੋਵੈ ਪਰਵਾਣੁ ਤਾ ਖਸਮੈ ਕਾ ਮਹਲੁ ਪਾਇਸੀ ॥
lines.translit: hukam maniai hovai paravaan taa khasamai kaa mehal paaeisee |
lines.english: Obeying the Order of His Will, he becomes acceptable, and then, he obtains the Mansion of the Lord's Presence.
Salok, First Mehl:
Then, he goes to the Court of the Lord, wearing robes of honor. ||15||

lines.gurmukhi: ਖਸਮੈ ਭਾਵੈ ਸੋ ਕਰੇ ਮਨਹੁ ਚਿੰਦਿਆ ਸੋ ਫਲੁ ਪਾਇਸੀ ॥
lines.translit: khasamai bhaavai so kare manahu chindiaa so fal paaeisee |
lines.english: One who acts to please His Lord and Master, obtains the fruits of his mind's desires.

lines.gurmukhi: ਧੋਤੀ ਟਿਕਾ ਤੈ ਜਪਮਾਲੀ ਧਾਨੁ ਮਲੇਛਾਂ ਖਾਈ ॥
lines.translit: dhotee ttikaa tai japamaalee dhaan malechhaan khaaee |
lines.english: They wear their loin cloths, apply ritual frontal marks to their foreheads, and carry their rosaries, but they eat food with the Muslims.

lines.gurmukhi: ਗਊ ਬਿਰਾਹਮਣ ਕਉ ਕਰੁ ਲਾਵਹੁ ਗੋਬਰਿ ਤਰਣੁ ਨ ਜਾਈ ॥
lines.translit: gaoo biraahaman kau kar laavahu gobar taran na jaaee |
lines.english: They tax the cows and the Brahmins, but the cow-dung they apply to their kitchen will not save them.
Taking the Naam, the Name of the Lord, you shall swim across. ||1||

lines.gurmukhi: ਅੰਤਰਿ ਪੂਜਾ ਪੜਹਿ ਕਤੇਬਾ ਸੰਜਮੁ ਤੁਰਕਾ ਭਾਈ ॥
lines.translit: antar poojaa parreh katebaa sanjam turakaa bhaaee |
lines.english: O Siblings of Destiny, you perform devotional worship indoors, but read the Islamic sacred texts, and adopt the Muslim way of life.
Renounce your hypocrisy!
The man-eaters say their prayers.
Those who wield the knife wear the sacred thread around their necks.
In their homes, the Brahmins sound the conch.
First Mehl:
Speaking falsehood, they take their food.
They too have the same taste.
The home of modesty and Dharma is far from them.
False is their capital, and false is their trade.
O Nanak, they are totally permeated with falsehood.
The sacred marks are on their foreheads, and the saffron loin-cloths are around their waists;
in their hands they hold the knives - they are the butchers of the world!
